tweet: Every elected official, every cabinet member, and others in power who stand idly by while this President tramples on American values and the Constitution is complicit. This is a moment of truth.

tweet: These are not the acts of strong leader; they reveal the desperate nature of a small man who cannot understand or comprehend what our nation needs.

tweet: The President then continued to reveal his true colors by threatening Americans across the country with military action. Absent the request of a Governor, this brazen act is appalling and completely upends American legal and constitutional norms.

tweet: Protesters are crying out to be heard – to demand an end to the bitter fruits of racism in law enforcement, health care, jobs and the economy. It’s fallen on deaf ears and a hard heart at the White House.

name: Rep. David E. Price

tweet: The other affected states had to wait 18 months, which was bad enough, but Puerto Rico is STILL waiting...  ICYMI: In PR—Nearly 3,000 people lost their lives, and tens of thousands of homes were damaged. I've been to PR and seen these challenges first hand.

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: But that's not even the full story, rather than comply with the clear statutory deadline (included in bipartisan legislation signed by the President) to issue the Fed Reg notice, @HUDgov is still withholding the notice for PUERTO RICO.

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: Low and middle-income families can’t wait 547 days to rebuild after a devastating storm.

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: Issuing the Federal Register notice has taken longer in recent years.  After Sandy in 2012, it took 35 days.  After the 2017 hurricanes, it took 145 days.  And more recently it took 547 days, and even then the notice came only after Congress included a deadline by law.

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: Without this notice, states (like NC) cannot submit action plans to @Hudgov, sign a grant agreement, and begin to use the $—necessary steps that take at least several months once the notice is issued.

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: Unfortunately, Congress has never formally approved the CDBG-DR program.   This means each time Congress gives $, @HUDgov must issue a notice in the Federal Register that outlines how the funds can be used.

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: In 2017 alone, Congress set aside more than $35 billion across the federal government for people recovering from major hurricanes: Harvey, Irma, & Maria.  To put this into perspective, HUD’s entire budget in the last fiscal year was approximately $54 billion.

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: Since 1993, Congress has provided more than $86 billion in funding for the CDBG-DR program.  As the severity and frequency of storms have increased, so has the need for funding. *climate change*

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: The grants primarily benefit low- and moderate-income people in the most impacted and distressed areas.

name: Rep. David E. Price
tweet: .@HUDgov’s Community Development Block Grant-Disaster Recovery program (CDBG-DR) is a flexible & vital tool for states & municipalities to recover from major disasters. Grantees use this funding to reconstruct damaged housing & repair public facilities such as community centers.
